title: Strength Tarot Card Meaning & Best Crystals
name: Strength
theme: Courage through softness, patience, inner fortitude
upright_meaning: A woman calmly opens the jaws of a lion with bare hands, often with an infinity symbol above her head, flowers forming a wreath in her hair and around the lion's neck. Strength is the archetype of power through gentleness — courage that tames not by force but by patient presence. Upright, it speaks to mastering the raw instinct within (fear, anger, appetite) through compassion rather than suppression.
reversed_meaning: Reversed, Strength often points to the shadow of inner fortitude — self-doubt that underestimates your capacity, raw impulse overriding hard-won mastery, or the burnout of pretending to be strong. It is an invitation to ask where you have stopped being gentle with yourself in the name of being tough.
psychological_lens: emotional regulation (the skill of meeting intense feeling with awareness rather than suppression or explosion)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: Strength's 'gentle taming of the lion' resonates with the Eastern contemplative principle of taming the mind through non-violent attention — strength that does not wage war on itself.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Tiger Eye, used across traditions as a stone of courage and integrity, embodies Strength's measured power — brave without being brutal.

recommended_practice: name one raw impulse you usually suppress or vent, and meet it today with one minute of patient attention instead

best_overall.name: Tiger Eye
best_overall.reason: Tiger Eye is the stone of balanced courage — brave without aggression, the gentle power this card names. Hold it when you need to meet fear or anger with presence rather than force; it anchors the patient mastery Strength depicts.

best_upright.name: Sunstone
best_upright.reason: Sunstone carries the warm, Leo-ruled radiance of healthy self-worth. For an upright Strength draw, it supports the confidence that lets you face what is hard without flinching.

best_reversed.name: Citrine
best_reversed.reason: When Strength reverses into self-doubt or burnout, Citrine restores the solar, confident energy that has dimmed. It is the stone for remembering your own capability when self-worth has dipped.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz brings compassion to Strength's love readings, where the instinct to 'manage' a partner can override tenderness. It invites patience with the other's raw edges — and your own.

best_daily_wear.name: Carnelian
best_daily_wear.reason: Carnelian is a daily stone of courage and grounded confidence. Worn as jewelry, it keeps Strength's gentle power within reach through the workday — a quiet reminder of inner fortitude.

content:
When Strength appears in a reading, it often reframes what power actually looks like — not force applied, but the gentle, patient mastery that tames what is wild without wounding it. Card 8 of the Major Arcana, it is the archetype of inner fortitude. Paired with the right crystals, this card becomes an invitation to meet fear, anger, or appetite with compassionate presence rather than suppression, and to find the kind of courage that does not require you to be hard.

Strength at a Glance

Strength is the card of courage, patience, compassion, and inner fortitude. Upright, it points to power expressed through gentleness; reversed, it often asks where self-doubt or burnout has dimmed your resilience. The crystal most often paired with Strength is Tiger Eye, the stone of balanced courage.

  • Archetype: The Gentle Power
  • Core theme: Courage through softness, patience, inner fortitude
  • Recommended crystal: Tiger Eye
  • Upright: courage, patience, compassion
  • Reversed: self-doubt, raw instinct over mastery, burnout

Strength Upright: Meaning & Message

In the Rider-Waite image, a woman in a flowing white gown calmly opens the jaws of a lion with her bare hands — not by force, but by a quiet, steady presence. An infinity symbol loops above her head. A wreath of flowers crowns her hair and forms a chain draped around the lion's neck and tail. Mountains rise behind them both. The lion, the symbol of raw instinct and appetite, leans into her hand rather than fighting it. Upright, Strength is the archetype of gentle power. The courage it depicts is not the absence of fear but its mastery — not by suppressing the lion (which only makes it stronger) but by meeting it with enough patient presence that it chooses to soften. In everyday terms, Strength often appears when you are facing something that tempts either suppression (locking the feeling away) or explosion (letting the lion loose), and the card offers a third path: holding the feeling with awareness until it shifts. This is also a card of self-mastery rather than other-control. The lion is within. Strength is the slow, patient work of befriending your own fear, anger, hunger, or grief — of becoming the calm hand on your own jaws.

Strength Reversed: The Shadow Aspect

When Strength appears reversed, the same inner fortitude has gone somewhere shadowed. The familiar form is self-doubt — the underestimating of your own capacity, the assumption that you cannot handle what is in front of you when, in fact, you have handled harder. Its opposite is raw instinct overriding mastery — the lion off the leash, appetite or anger running the show after a long period of holding it together. A third form is burnout — the exhaustion that comes from being strong for too long without rest. This is not a verdict of weakness. A reversed card is information for reflection. Strength reversed often asks one honest question: where have you stopped being gentle with yourself in the name of being tough — and what is the cost?

A reflection prompt: Name one raw impulse you usually either suppress or vent — fear, anger, appetite, grief. Today, try meeting it for one minute with patient attention instead: neither pushing it away nor letting it run. Strength is built in that minute.

Best Crystals for Strength

Crystals paired with Strength tend to share qualities of balanced courage, warm confidence, or patient endurance, matching the card's theme of gentle power. The five below are drawn from the major crystal-and-tarot references and mineral tradition, chosen for distinct aspects of this card — from the balanced-courage stone itself, to a stone of solar self-worth, to a stone that restores confidence when it has dimmed.

Best Overall Crystal for Strength

Tiger Eye is the stone of balanced courage — brave without aggression, the gentle power this card names. Hold it when you need to meet fear or anger with presence rather than force; it anchors the patient mastery Strength depicts, the steady hand on the lion's jaws that does not need to grip hard to hold.

Tiger Eye — Tiger Eye is the stone of balanced courage — brave without aggression, the gentle power this card names. Hold it when you need to meet fear or anger with presence rather than force; it anchors the patient mastery Strength depicts. Read Tiger Eye Meaning → · Shop Tiger Eye →

Best Crystal for Upright Strength Energy

Sunstone carries the warm, Leo-ruled radiance of healthy self-worth — labyrinthos lists it for Strength. For an upright draw, when the lion is being met with patient presence, Sunstone supports the confidence that lets you face what is hard without flinching or pretending it is easy.

Sunstone — Sunstone carries the warm, Leo-ruled radiance of healthy self-worth. For an upright Strength draw, it supports the confidence that lets you face what is hard without flinching. Browse Sunstone jewelry →

Best Crystal for Reversed Strength (Shadow Work)

When Strength reverses into self-doubt or burnout, Citrine restores the solar, confident energy that has dimmed. It is the stone for remembering your own capability when self-worth has dipped — for the recognition that you have handled hard things before and will handle this one too.

Citrine — When Strength reverses into self-doubt or burnout, Citrine restores the solar, confident energy that has dimmed. It is the stone for remembering your own capability when self-worth has dipped. Citrine properties & symbolism → · Find your Citrine piece →

Best Crystal for Love & Relationship Readings

Rose Quartz brings compassion to Strength's love readings, where the instinct to "manage" a partner (or to be managed by one) can override tenderness. It invites patience with the other's raw edges — and your own — so the relationship is held with the same gentle mastery this card depicts.

Rose Quartz — Rose Quartz brings compassion to Strength's love readings, where the instinct to 'manage' a partner can override tenderness. It invites patience with the other's raw edges — and your own. Read Rose Quartz Meaning → · Shop Rose Quartz →

Best Crystal to Wear Daily

Carnelian is a daily stone of courage and grounded confidence. Worn as a bracelet, it keeps Strength's gentle power within reach through the workday — a quiet reminder of inner fortitude that does not need to announce itself to be real.

Using Strength Crystals in Your Tarot Practice

A short practice to bring Strength's energy into your reading or your day. Keep it practical — crystals are a focus for intention, not magic.

You'll need: Tiger Eye or Sunstone.

Step 1 — Set the focus (1 min): Name one raw impulse you are currently living with — fear, anger, an appetite, a grief. Acknowledge it without judgment and without acting on it yet. Strength begins by admitting the lion is there, rather than pretending it is not.

Step 2 — Hold the stone + draw a card (2 min): Hold your Tiger Eye over your solar plexus and draw a card with the question "what does this feeling need from me?" Let the stone anchor you in steady presence as you sit with the answer — Strength is the minute spent holding the impulse with awareness before deciding what to do with it.

Step 3 — Close with one commitment: Close by naming one small, kind action you can take toward yourself today — rest, water, a walk, a boundary held — that honors what the feeling is telling you. Place the stone somewhere visible until you take it. Strength includes gentleness with yourself, not only endurance.

Prefer something quicker? Touch your Carnelian jewelry each morning and ask: "Where might I meet something difficult today with patience instead of force?" The card's gift is gentle power; this small ritual trains the habit of responding rather than reacting.

Crystals to Support Strength Reversed

When Strength appears reversed, it often points to self-doubt, raw instinct over mastery, burnout — the card's energy in shadow rather than a bad omen. It's an invitation to notice where courage and patience has gone into overdrive or gone quiet. The crystal below is traditionally used to support this specific reflection.

Supporting stone — Citrine: Citrine is the steady stone for the shadow work this reversal invites. When you notice self-doubt dimming your sense of what you can handle, hold a piece over your solar plexus and let its warm, solar energy recall you to your actual capacity — the evidence, accumulated over years, that you have met hard things before. Pair it with the Rose Quartz above if the self-doubt has an emotional source; together they restore confidence while they soften the inner critic.

Strength Through Three Lenses

1. Tarot tradition: In the Rider-Waite tradition, Strength is numbered 8 (in the most common ordering) and corresponds to Leo. It is read as the archetype of courage through compassion — the complement to The Chariot, where triumph came from directing outward force. Where The Chariot masters the world, Strength masters the self. The infinity symbol above her head marks the endless, renewable nature of this kind of power.

2. Psychological archetype: In psychological terms, Strength maps closely onto the skill of emotional regulation — the capacity, well-studied in research on resilience, to meet intense feeling with awareness rather than with suppression (which bottlenecks) or explosion (which damages). The most effective regulation is not the absence of feeling but the gentle, patient attention that lets a feeling move through without taking over. Read through this lens, Strength is not asking you to be tough. It is asking you to be present — and presence, sustained, turns out to be far more powerful than force.

This lens treats Strength as a mirror for self-inquiry — the psychological dimension here is emotional regulation — rather than a prediction.

3. Crystal companion: As a crystal companion, Tiger Eye becomes the tangible anchor for the balanced courage this card asks. Strength reminds you that mastery is gentle; the stone gives you a focus to hold while you practice it. Worn as jewelry, it is a small daily cue to meet the difficult thing with presence rather than with a braced defense — the kind of quiet, renewable power the infinity symbol above the figure's head actually depicts.

Strength in the Eastern Tradition

Strength's "gentle taming of the lion" resonates deeply with the Eastern contemplative principle of taming the mind through non-violent attention. The Tibetan Buddhist traditions speak of working with the difficult energies (fear, anger, desire) not by waging war on them but by meeting them with steady, compassionate awareness until they lose their grip. The lion is not killed; it is befriended. For Strength, this echo reframes the card's courage as the strength that does not wage war on itself — the kind that endures precisely because it does not exhaust.

Tiger Eye, used across traditions as a stone of courage and integrity, embodies Strength's measured power — brave without being brutal, steady without being rigid. The stone's characteristic shifting flash (chatoyancy) has carried the symbolism across cultures of seeing clearly in shifting conditions. For this card, the cross-cultural reverence for Tiger Eye as the balanced-courage stone pairs naturally with the Western crystal-and-tarot reading of it as the anchor of gentle mastery.

A Mini Crystal Tarot Spread for Strength

A three-card spread to explore Strength's message in your life, with a crystal paired to each position.

PositionQuestionCompanion Crystal
1. Where Strength is showing upWhere is The Gentle Power energy active in my life right now?Tiger Eye
2. What it invitesWhat is this card asking me to notice or do?Sunstone
3. The next stepWhat small action aligns me with this card's healthy expression?Carnelian

Shuffle your deck, place a stone on each position as you draw, and sit with the three cards together for a few minutes before journaling.

Tarot and crystal meanings are based on spiritual traditions, symbolism, and personal mindfulness practices. They are a tool for self-reflection and contemplation, not a substitute for medical, financial, or professional advice — and not a prediction of fixed outcomes.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does Strength mean in tarot?Strength (card 8 of the Major Arcana) is the card of courage, patience, compassion, and inner fortitude. It corresponds to Leo and is traditionally read as the archetype of power expressed through gentleness — the taming of raw instinct not by force but by patient presence. In a reading, it often points to self-mastery and the value of meeting difficulty with compassion.
What is the upright meaning of Strength?Upright, Strength points to courage expressed as gentle, patient mastery. It suggests that the way through a current difficulty is not suppression or force but steady, compassionate presence — meeting fear, anger, or appetite with awareness until it softens. It is often read as a sign of inner resilience, and a reminder that real strength includes kindness toward yourself.
What are the best crystals for Strength?The crystals most often paired with Strength are Tiger Eye (for balanced courage), Sunstone (for the solar self-worth of Leo), Citrine (for restoring confidence when it has dimmed), Rose Quartz (for compassion in love), and Carnelian (for daily grounded courage). The principle across references is steady, warm, non-aggressive power.
What does Strength mean in love?In love readings, Strength often points to the patient compassion a relationship requires — meeting a partner's difficult moments with steady presence rather than reactivity, and receiving the same in return. It can also flag the shadow of suppressing your own needs to keep the peace. The invitation is gentle honesty rather than managed calm.
What does Strength reversed mean?Reversed, Strength often points to one of three shadow patterns: self-doubt (underestimating your capacity), raw impulse overriding mastery (the lion off the leash), or burnout (exhaustion from being strong too long). It is not a bad card — it is an invitation to ask where you have stopped being gentle with yourself in the name of being tough, and to restore the balance.
What chakra is Strength associated with?The crystals paired with Strength work primarily with the Solar Plexus and Heart chakras — Tiger Eye, Sunstone, and Carnelian for the courage and confidence of the Solar Plexus, and Rose Quartz for the compassion of the Heart. The card's energy lives where willpower and warmth meet.
Is Strength a bad card?In tarot, no card is inherently 'good' or 'bad' — every card, including reversed positions, is read as information for reflection rather than a fixed outcome. For Strength, the card's "gentle mastery" can read as self-doubt or suppressed struggle when reversed — but the invitation is simply to restore kindness toward yourself along with the courage, not to treat the reversed card as a sign that you are fundamentally weak.
Can tarot predict the future?No — tarot is a symbolic system used for self-reflection and contemplation, not a tool that predicts fixed outcomes. The cards surface patterns and questions; your choices shape what happens next. For Strength, the card may point to the patience a situation asks of you — but whether you meet it with gentle presence or with force remains entirely in your hands.

Related Tarot Cards

  • The Chariot: The Victor — Will, direction, triumph through focused effort
  • The Sun: The Radiant — Joy, vitality, clarity, success
  • All Tarot Card Crystals → (Hub)

Strength is the card of the courage that does not need to be hard. Pair it with a crystal that supports balanced bravery, and let it be a reminder that the wildest parts of you are not enemies to be conquered but companions to be met with patient, compassionate presence — and that this, more than any force, is what real strength actually looks like.
